decisions.decision: Use vi.useFakeTimers() with beforeEach/afterEach pattern (basic, no shouldAdvanceTime) for formatRelativeTime and timeUntil tests
alternatives_rejected: shouldAdvanceTime option — unnecessary for pure unit tests that only need pinned Date.now()
Shared test helper — project has no shared time helper; each file manages independently

decisions.decision: Test that negative numbers preserve sign through K/M formatting (e.g., -45000 → '-45.0K')
alternatives_rejected: Only test positive numbers — would miss verifying the Math.abs threshold vs signed output behavior

decisions.decision: Add all 10 new tests to the existing frontend/src/__tests__/format.test.ts file, adding new describe blocks for formatRelativeTime and timeUntil
alternatives_rejected: Separate test file — existing file already tests all format.ts exports; keeping tests co-located is consistent

steps.task: Add formatRelativeTime and timeUntil imports to format.test.ts

Add describe('formatRelativeTime') block with vi.useFakeTimers() in beforeEach (pinned to a known date) and vi.useRealTimers() in afterEach. Add 4 tests: 'just now' (<1 min), minutes ago, hours ago, days ago
Add describe('timeUntil') block with same fake timer pattern. Add 3 tests: expired (past date), hours+minutes remaining, days+hours remaining
Add negative number test to existing formatCurrency describe block: -45000 → '-45.0K', -1500000 → '-1.5M'
Add 2 tests to existing truncateHash describe block: custom length param (e.g., truncateHash('sha256:abcdef0123456789', 4)), and non-sha256 long string truncation
Run vitest on format.test.ts to verify all 19 tests (9 existing + 10 new) pass
